title: Full‑bodied Filipino Peanuts Stew
description: A nutty, velvety stew that soothes hangovers, blending rice wine, coconut milk, and ground peanuts in a hot‑pot vessel.
instructions: Heat the hot‑pot vessel over medium heat and add the coconut milk and rice wine.
Stir until the mixture comes to a gentle simmer, then lower the heat.
Add the diced onion, minced garlic, sliced ginger, and brown sugar; cook until fragrant, about 2 minutes.
Pour in the chicken broth and fish sauce, then bring the stew to a low boil.
Reduce the heat to a simmer and stir in the whole peanuts, ensuring they coat in the liquid.
Add the shrimp; simmer until the shrimp are pink and cooked through, about 5 minutes.
Stir in the spinach leaves and cook until wilted, about 2 minutes.
Season with black pepper to taste and adjust seasoning with extra fish sauce if needed.
Transfer the stew to individual serving bowls and garnish with toasted coconut flakes if desired.
Serve hot, ideally with steamed rice or fresh bread to soak up the velvety sauce.
